title: ONGC Geology — Full-length Mock 03
pattern: ONGC CBT — 85 MCQ · 120 min · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The zeolite facies represents metamorphism at which approximate P-T conditions?
options: Low temperature and low pressure (200–350°C, 1–3 kbar)
High temperature and high pressure (600–800°C, 8–12 kbar)
Moderate temperature and high pressure (400–500°C, 5–8 kbar)
Very high temperature (>900°C) at any pressure
questions.solution: The zeolite facies occupies the lowest-grade region of metamorphic facies space, typically at 200–350°C and 1–3 kbar. It is characterized by zeolite minerals (laumontite, analcime) and is common in burial metamorphism of volcanic and sedimentary rocks.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Which index mineral marks the beginning of medium-grade regional metamorphism in pelitic rocks?
questions.solution: Garnet marks the transition from low-grade to medium-grade metamorphism in pelitic rocks (Barrovian sequence). The typical sequence is chlorite → biotite → garnet → staurolite → kyanite → sillimanite.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Al₂SiO₅ phase diagram shows three polymorphs. Kyanite is the stable phase at:
options: High pressure and relatively low temperature
Very low pressure at all temperatures
questions.solution: Kyanite is the high-pressure polymorph of Al₂SiO₅, stable at pressures above ~3.8 kbar. Sillimanite is stable at high temperatures, and andalusite is stable at low pressures. The triple point is at approximately 500°C, 3.8 kbar.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The foliation in a schist is primarily defined by:
options: Alignment of platy and elongate minerals such as micas and amphiboles
Alternating light and dark bands of quartz and feldspar
questions.solution: Schistosity is a pervasive foliation defined by the preferred orientation of medium- to coarse-grained phyllosilicates (micas, chlorite) and elongate minerals (amphiboles) that grow or rotate perpendicular to the maximum compressive stress.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A hornfels is typically formed by which type of metamorphism?
questions.solution: Hornfels is a fine-grained, hard, non-foliated rock formed by contact (thermal) metamorphism near an igneous intrusion. The high temperature recrystallizes the country rock without significant directed pressure, producing a characteristic granoblastic texture.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The metamorphic equivalent of a basaltic protolith at amphibolite facies conditions is:
questions.solution: Basalt (a mafic igneous rock) metamorphosed at amphibolite facies (500–700°C, 3–10 kbar) recrystallizes to an amphibolite dominated by hornblende and plagioclase. The mineral assemblage reflects the bulk composition of the mafic protolith.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Dynamic recrystallization during metamorphism produces:
options: Strained, elongated grains with undulose extinction
Equigranular, strain-free grains in high-strain zones
questions.solution: Dynamic recrystallization occurs during deformation and produces new, strain-free grains that replace deformed ones. It is characteristic of mylonites and ductile shear zones, creating fine-grained, equigranular aggregates.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The breakdown of staurolite in metapelites marks the transition from:
questions.solution: Staurolite breaks down at approximately 600–650°C, marking the upper boundary of medium-grade metamorphism. The reaction staurolite + quartz → kyanite + cordierite + H₂O marks the transition to high-grade (granulite facies) conditions.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Retrograde metamorphism refers to:
options: Metamorphism caused by fluid infiltration only
questions.solution: Retrograde metamorphism occurs as rocks are exhumed and cooled, producing lower-grade mineral assemblages that partially or wholly replace peak metamorphic minerals. It often involves fluid-assisted reactions producing sericite, chlorite, and clay.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Which facies represents the highest-grade metamorphic conditions?
questions.solution: The granulite facies represents the highest-grade regional metamorphism at approximately 700–900°C and 5–12 kbar. It is characterized by the absence of hydrous minerals (biotite, amphibole) and the presence of pyroxene, garnet, and feldspar.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Basaltic magma has a SiO₂ content of approximately:
questions.solution: Basaltic (mafic) magma contains 45–52% SiO₂. It is characterized by low viscosity, high temperature (~1100–1250°C), and high content of Mg, Fe, and Ca. It erupts effusively as lava flows.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A porphyritic texture in an igneous rock indicates:
options: Rapid cooling throughout the entire crystallization history
Two-stage cooling: slow at depth followed by rapid eruption at the surface
questions.solution: A porphyritic texture develops when magma begins cooling slowly at depth (forming large phenocrysts) and then erupts or is intruded shallowly, causing rapid cooling of the remaining melt (forming fine-grained groundmass).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In Bowen's Reaction Series, which mineral forms on the continuous (plagioclase) reaction series at the highest temperature?
questions.solution: The continuous reaction series involves plagioclase feldspar crystallizing from Ca-rich (anorthite) at ~1555°C to Na-rich (albite) at ~1100°C. Anorthite is the first plagioclase to crystallize at the highest temperature.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Obsidian is a volcanic glass that forms when:
options: Magma cools extremely rapidly, preventing crystallization
questions.solution: Obsidian forms when silica-rich (rhyolitic) lava cools so rapidly that atoms cannot organize into crystal lattices, resulting in a volcanic glass. It typically has a conchoidal fracture and dark color despite high SiO₂ content.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Fractional crystallization of a basaltic magma will produce a residual liquid enriched in:
questions.solution: As a basaltic magma crystallizes, early-forming mafic minerals (olivine, pyroxene) remove Mg and Fe. The residual melt becomes progressively enriched in SiO₂, alkalis (Na₂O, K₂O), and volatiles, potentially evolving toward andesitic, dacitic, or rhyolitic compositions.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The plutonic equivalent of basalt is:
questions.solution: Gabbro is the coarse-grained (plutonic) equivalent of basalt (fine-grained, volcanic). Both have the same basaltic (mafic) composition with 45–52% SiO₂, dominated by plagioclase and pyroxene.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Andesitic volcanism is most characteristic of which tectonic setting?
options: Subduction zones (island arcs and continental arcs)
questions.solution: Andesitic magma is the hallmark of subduction zones where oceanic lithosphere subducts, releases water into the mantle wedge, triggers partial melting, and produces intermediate (andesitic) magmas at continental arcs (Andes) and island arcs (Japan).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A shield volcano is characterized by:
options: Steep-sided, explosive eruptions producing pyroclastic deposits
Gentle slopes built by fluid basaltic lava flows
questions.solution: Shield volcanoes (e.g., Mauna Loa, Hawaii) have broad, gently sloping profiles (5–10°) built by successive eruptions of low-viscosity basaltic lava that flows great distances from the vent.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Lamprophyres are distinguished from other mafic igneous rocks by their:
options: Low silica content and abundance of phenocrystic mafic minerals (biotite, amphibole, olivine) in a fine-grained groundmass
High silica content and granite-like mineralogy
questions.solution: Lamprophyres are volatile-rich, ultrapotassic to potassic mafic rocks characterized by euhedral phenocrysts of mafic minerals (biotite, amphibole, olivine, pyroxene) in a fine-grained groundmass. They are derived from enriched mantle sources.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A pyroclastic flow is a hot mixture of:
options: Gas, volcanic ash, pumice fragments, and rock debris moving at high speed
Only volcanic gases and fine ash
questions.solution: Pyroclastic flows (nuées ardentes) are density currents of hot gas, ash, pumice, and lithic fragments that travel at speeds up to 700 km/h at temperatures of 200–700°C. They are among the most deadly volcanic hazards.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a normal fault, the hanging wall moves:
questions.solution: A normal fault is characterized by the hanging wall moving downward relative to the footwall, typically caused by extensional (tensional) stress. It is common at divergent plate boundaries and continental rift zones.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A thrust fault has a dip of less than:
questions.solution: A thrust fault is a reverse fault with a dip of less than 45°, typically 10–30°. It is produced by compressional stress and is responsible for large-scale crustal shortening in mountain belts (e.g., Himalayas, Alps).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Columnar basalt joints form due to:
options: Thermal contraction during cooling of lava
questions.solution: Columnar joints form as basaltic lava cools and contracts, creating regular hexagonal (or polygonal) fracture patterns perpendicular to the cooling surface. Examples include the Giant's Causeway (Ireland) and Devil's Postpile (California).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Mohr's circle is used in structural geology to:
options: Determine the age of rock formations
Visualize the state of stress at a point and find principal stresses
questions.solution: Mohr's circle graphically represents the normal and shear stresses on any plane at a point. The circle's intersections with the horizontal axis give the principal stresses (σ₁ and σ₃), and the maximum shear stress equals the circle's radius.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: On a lower-hemisphere equal-area (Schmidt) stereonet, a vertical plane is represented by:
options: A great circle passing through the centre of the net
A great circle that coincides with the primitive (outer) circle
A single point at the centre
A small circle near the pole
questions.solution: A vertical plane (90° dip) strikes along the primitive circle on a lower-hemisphere stereonet. Its pole would lie on the primitive circle itself. Horizontal planes plot as the centre point.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A mylonite is characterized by:
options: Randomly oriented crystals with no preferred fabric
Intense ductile deformation producing fine-grained, foliated, and often lineated rock in a shear zone
Angular fragments in a fine-grained matrix produced by brittle fracture
Coarse-grained metamorphic minerals with no deformation features
questions.solution: Mylonites form by intense ductile deformation within shear zones. Dynamic recrystallization reduces grain size, and strong shearing produces a strong foliation (mylonitic foliation) and lineation (stretching lineation).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Slaty cleavage forms perpendicular to the direction of:
questions.solution: Slatey cleavage develops perpendicular to the maximum compressive stress (σ₁). During low-grade metamorphism, platy minerals (clay → chlorite → mica) align perpendicular to compression, and pressure solution removes material from high-stress grain contacts.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: An angular unconformity implies that:
options: Sediments were deposited continuously without any break
Older rocks below the unconformity were tilted or folded before erosion and renewed deposition
Both rock units have the same dip but different ages
No erosion occurred between the two rock units
questions.solution: An angular unconformity involves a significant time gap during which the older strata were deformed (tilted or folded), eroded, and then younger horizontal strata were deposited on top. The most famous example is Siccar Point (Hutton's Unconformity) in Scotland.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The axial surface of a fold connects:
options: The outer edges of the fold limbs
The hinge lines of successive folded surfaces
The points of maximum strain on each limb
The intersection of the fold with the ground surface
questions.solution: The axial surface (axial plane) is the surface that connects the hinge lines of successive folded layers. It divides the fold into two limbs and its orientation is fundamental for classifying folds (upright, inclined, recumbent).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Boudinage structures indicate which type of deformation?
options: Layer-parallel extension causing competent layers to thin and break into boudins
questions.solution: Boudinage forms when a competent layer (e.g., quartz vein, sandstone) is stretched parallel to its length. The competent layer necks and breaks into sausage-shaped blocks (boudins) while the incompetent matrix flows into the gaps.

figure.caption: Boudinage showing a competent layer stretched and broken into boudins

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: P-waves (primary waves) in seismology are:
options: Transverse waves that cannot travel through liquids
Longitudinal (compressional) waves that can travel through solids and liquids
Surface waves that travel only along the Earth's surface
Shear waves with the highest velocity
questions.solution: P-waves are compressional (longitudinal) waves where particle motion is parallel to the direction of wave propagation. They travel through all materials (solids, liquids, gases) and are the fastest seismic waves.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: S-waves (secondary waves) cannot travel through:
questions.solution: S-waves are shear waves that require a rigid medium to propagate. They cannot travel through the liquid outer core, creating the seismic shadow zone. This observation was key evidence that the outer core is liquid.

figure.caption: Seismic wave shadow zones showing P-wave and S-wave paths through Earth's interior

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A gravity anomaly that is negative over a basin suggests:
options: The basin fill is less dense than the surrounding basement rock
The basin fill is denser than the surrounding basement rock
No density contrast exists between the basin and basement
The basin is deeper than 10 km
questions.solution: A negative gravity anomaly over a sedimentary basin indicates that the basin fill (sediments) has a lower density than the surrounding crystalline basement. The Bouguer anomaly is negative because there is a mass deficiency relative to the reference.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnetic surveys in mineral exploration are most effective for detecting rocks containing:
options: Magnetite, pyrrhotite, and other ferromagnetic minerals
questions.solution: Magnetic surveys detect variations in the Earth's magnetic field caused by ferromagnetic minerals (magnetite, titanomagnetite, pyrrhotite). They are used to map igneous intrusions, ore bodies, and basement structures beneath sedimentary cover.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a Vertical Electrical Sounding (VES), increasing the electrode spacing allows investigation of:
questions.solution: VES uses the Wenner or Schlumberger array with progressively wider electrode spacing to investigate increasing depths. Current penetrates deeper as the electrode spacing increases, allowing layered Earth models to be constructed.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A spontaneous potential (SP) log in a borehole primarily measures:
options: Natural gamma radiation from the formation
Natural electrical potential differences between borehole fluid and formation water
questions.solution: The SP log records naturally occurring electrical potentials generated by electrochemical and electrokinetic processes at shale/sand interfaces and between borehole mud and formation water. It is used to identify permeable zones and estimate formation water resistivity.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A bright spot on a seismic reflection profile may indicate:
options: A gas-charged sandstone reservoir (low impedance contrast)
questions.solution: Bright spots are high-amplitude seismic reflections caused by a strong impedance decrease, commonly associated with gas-bearing sands. The gas reduces both density and velocity, creating a high reflection coefficient at the top of the reservoir.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Bouguer gravity anomaly is corrected for:
options: Latitude, free-air, and the gravitational effect of terrain between station and sea level
Only the elevation of the station
questions.solution: The Bouguer anomaly = observed gravity − theoretical gravity + free-air correction − Bouguer slab correction + terrain correction. It removes the effects of latitude, elevation, and topography, leaving anomalies caused by subsurface density variations.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Airborne gamma-ray spectrometry measures the natural radioactivity of:
options: The upper ~30 cm of the Earth's surface (K, U, Th concentrations)
Deep crustal rocks at >1 km depth
questions.solution: Gamma-ray spectrometry detects gamma radiation emitted by ⁴⁰K, ²³⁸U, and ²³²Th decay series in the upper ~30 cm of the surface. It is used for geological mapping, mineral exploration, and environmental studies.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR) is most effective in:
options: Dry, resistive materials like sand, gravel, and dry rock
questions.solution: GPR works best in dry, low-conductivity materials where electromagnetic waves can penetrate. Wet clays and saline groundwater attenuate GPR signals rapidly, limiting penetration depth to less than 1 m in some cases.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Based on the gas production line graph, what is the average annual growth rate in gas production from 2019 to 2024?
questions.solution: Growth from 18 BCM (2019) to 29 BCM (2024) over 5 years. Using CAGR formula: CAGR = (29/18)^(1/5) − 1 = (1.611)^(0.2) − 1 ≈ 1.100 − 1 = 10.0%.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If the gas production continues increasing by the same absolute amount each year, what will be the production in 2025?
questions.solution: The absolute increase has been approximately 2 BCM per year (18→20→22→25→27→29). From 2024 to 2025, production = 29 + 2 = 31 BCM.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A well produces 120 barrels of oil per day. If 1 barrel ≈ 159 litres, how many litres does the well produce in 30 days?
questions.solution: Daily production = 120 × 159 = 19,080 litres. In 30 days = 19,080 × 30 = 572,400 litres.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A seismic crew operates 12 geophone stations, each recording at 500 Hz sampling rate. How many data points are collected per second from all stations combined?
questions.solution: Each station records 500 data points per second. With 12 stations: 500 × 12 = 6,000 data points per second.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Complete the sequence: Slate, Phyllite, Schist, Gneiss, ?
questions.solution: The sequence follows increasing metamorphic grade in pelitic rocks: Slate (very low grade) → Phyllite → Schist → Gneiss → Migmatite (partial melting at the highest grade).

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If PETROLOGY is coded as RWALUKVI, how is SEDIMENT coded?
questions.solution: Each letter is shifted by a fixed pattern. Applying the same shift pattern to SEDIMENT produces WKHMIRWV.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Rock : Mineral :: Soil : ?
questions.solution: A rock is composed of minerals, just as soil is derived from parent material (the underlying rock or sediment from which soil forms through weathering).

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A reservoir has an area of 5 km² and an average net pay thickness of 20 m. If porosity is 22% and water saturation is 30%, what is the hydrocarbon pore volume in million cubic metres?
questions.solution: Volume = Area × Thickness = 5,000,000 m² × 20 m = 100,000,000 m³. HC pore volume = 100 × 0.22 × (1 − 0.30) = 100 × 0.22 × 0.70 = 15.4 million m³.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Complete the series: P, Q, R, S, T, ?
questions.solution: The series is a simple alphabetical progression: P→Q→R→S→T→U. Each term is the next letter in the alphabet.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A seismic line is 40 km long with geophones spaced 50 m apart. How many geophone stations are needed?
questions.solution: Number of stations = (Length / Spacing) + 1 = (40,000 / 50) + 1 = 800 + 1 = 801 ≈ 800 stations.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The density of a rock is 2.65 g/cm³ and the density of water is 1.0 g/cm³. What is the specific gravity of the rock?
questions.solution: Specific gravity = Density of substance / Density of water = 2.65 / 1.0 = 2.65.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If ONGC's total expenditure was ₹62,000 Cr and the E&P share increases by 5 percentage points next year (total expenditure unchanged), what will be the new E&P expenditure?
questions.solution: New E&P share = 42% + 5% = 47%. New E&P expenditure = 47% of ₹62,000 Cr = 0.47 × 62,000 = ₹29,140 Cr.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If Africa's share of imports decreases by 5% next year while total imports remain the same, what will be the new Africa import volume?
questions.solution: Current Africa total = 25+22 = 47 MMT. A 5% decrease: 47 × 0.95 = 44.65 ≈ 44.5 MMT.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: In a code language, if MOUNTAIN is written as NPVUOJBO, how is VOLCANO written?
questions.solution: Each letter is shifted +1 position: M→N, O→P, U→V, N→O, T→U, A→B, I→J, N→O. Similarly: V→W, O→P, L→M, C→D, A→B, N→O, O→P = WPMDBOP.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: ONGC was incorporated in which year?
questions.solution: Oil and Natural Gas Commission (ONGC) was established on 14th August 1956. It was later converted to a corporation (ONGC) in 1994 and further to a public limited company (ONGC Videsh) in 2003.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The first commercial oil well in India was drilled at:
questions.solution: Digboi in Upper Assam is the site of India's first commercial oil well, drilled in 1889. It is one of the oldest operating oilfields in the world and is still in production.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's Deep Ocean Mission aims to explore minerals in which ocean basin?
questions.solution: India's Deep Ocean Mission (launched 2021) focuses on exploring polymetallic nodules in the Indian Ocean, particularly in the Central Indian Ocean Basin where India has a contract area of 75,000 km².

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Palk Strait separates India from:
questions.solution: The Palk Strait is a narrow body of water between the southeastern coast of Tamil Nadu, India, and the northern coast of Sri Lanka. Adam's Bridge (Rama Setu) connects the two countries across this strait.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Hydrocarbon Exploration Licensing Policy (HELP) was introduced in India in:
questions.solution: HELP was introduced in March 2016, replacing the earlier NELP (New Exploration Licensing Policy). It introduced the Open Acreage Licensing Policy (OALP) and the Unified License (ULP) for all hydrocarbons.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's renewable energy target for 2030 under the Paris Agreement commitments is:
questions.solution: India has committed to achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel-based energy capacity by 2030 under its updated NDC (Nationally Determined Contributions) to the Paris Agreement.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Rajya Sabha has a maximum strength of:
questions.solution: The Rajya Sabha (Council of States) has a maximum strength of 250 members, of which 238 are elected by state legislatures and 12 are nominated by the President. Currently, it has 245 members.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: ONGC's Mangalore refinery (MRPL) has a refining capacity of approximately:
questions.solution: Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals Limited (MRPL), a subsidiary of ONGC, has a refining capacity of approximately 15 million metric tonnes per annum (MMTPA) after its Phase III expansion.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: OPEC (Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries) was founded in:
questions.solution: OPEC was founded on 14 September 1960 in Baghdad by five founding members: Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela. It currently has 12 member countries.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: World Oil Conservation Day is observed on which date?
questions.solution: World Oil Conservation Day is observed on 14th March every year to promote awareness about the conservation of oil and energy resources for future generations.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the correct sentence:
options: The seismic survey were completed last week.
The seismic survey was completed last week.
The seismic survey has been completed last week.
The seismic survey is completed last week.
questions.solution: The simple past passive ('was completed') is correct for a completed action in the past. 'Survey' is singular, requiring 'was' instead of 'were'.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word closest in meaning to 'SEDIMENTARY':
options: Of or relating to metamorphic processes
Formed by the deposition and cementation of mineral or organic particles
questions.solution: Sedimentary refers to rocks formed by the accumulation, deposition, and cementation of sediments (mineral or organic particles) at the Earth's surface, typically in layers.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The crude oil was transported ______ pipeline to the refinery.'
questions.solution: 'By pipeline' is the standard English expression for indicating the mode of transport.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Identify the error: 'The number of exploratory wells have increased significantly this year.'
questions.solution: 'The number of' takes a singular verb because 'number' is the subject (not 'wells'). The correct form is 'The number of exploratory wells has increased'.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Read: 'The Deccan Traps, one of the largest volcanic features on Earth, consist of multiple layers of solidified flood basalt. They were formed at the end of the Cretaceous period.' What does 'consist of' mean?
options: Are made up of or composed of
questions.solution: 'Consist of' means to be composed or made up of something.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word closest in meaning to 'ABUNDANT':
questions.solution: Abundant means existing in large quantities; plentiful.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Convert to passive: 'The geophysicist interpreted the seismic data.'
options: The seismic data was interpreted by the geophysicist.
The seismic data has been interpreted by the geophysicist.
The seismic data is interpreted by the geophysicist.
The seismic data had been interpreted by the geophysicist.
questions.solution: Simple past active (interpreted) converts to 'was/were + past participle' in passive voice.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: What does the idiom 'hit bedrock' mean?
options: To reach the lowest point or minimum level
questions.solution: 'Hit bedrock' means to reach the lowest possible level or the absolute minimum.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the correct form: 'Each of the boreholes ______ been logged for porosity.'
questions.solution: 'Each' is singular and requires a singular verb: 'has'.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the sentence with correct punctuation and conjunction usage:
options: Since the formation was tight; permeability enhancement was required.
Since the formation was tight, permeability enhancement was required.
Since, the formation was tight permeability enhancement was required.
Since the formation was tight permeability, enhancement was required.
questions.solution: 'Since' is a subordinating conjunction. A comma should follow the dependent clause before the independent clause.

questions.solution: Q2 had the highest revenue at ₹1,640 Cr. Share = 1,640/6,240 × 100 ≈ 26.3%.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Study the line graph showing seismograph amplitude (mm) recorded at different distances (km) from a blast site. What is the approximate attenuation rate per km?

figure.caption: Seismic Amplitude vs Distance from Blast Site
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 500 280' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='500' height='280' fill='#f8fafc'/><text x='250' y='22' font-size='13' text-anchor='middle' font-weight='bold'>Seismic Amplitude vs Distance</text><line x1='70' y1='40' x2='70' y2='230' stroke='#64748b' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='70' y1='230' x2='460' y2='230' stroke='#64748b' stroke-width='2'/><polyline points='100,55 180,100 260,140 340,175 420,210' fill='none' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='3'/><circle cx='100' cy='55' r='4' fill='#dc2626'/><circle cx='180' cy='100' r='4' fill='#dc2626'/><circle cx='260' cy='140' r='4' fill='#dc2626'/><circle cx='340' cy='175' r='4' fill='#dc2626'/><circle cx='420' cy='210' r='4' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='100' y='48' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626' text-anchor='middle'>50</text><text x='180' y='93' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626' text-anchor='middle'>35</text><text x='260' y='133' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626' text-anchor='middle'>22</text><text x='340' y='168' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626' text-anchor='middle'>12</text><text x='420' y='203' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626' text-anchor='middle'>5</text><text x='100' y='250' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>1</text><text x='180' y='250' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>5</text><text x='260' y='250' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>10</text><text x='340' y='250' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>20</text><text x='420' y='250' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>30</text><text x='265' y='270' font-size='11' fill='#64748b' text-anchor='middle'>Distance (km)</text><text x='30' y='140' font-size='10' fill='#64748b' transform='rotate(-90,30,140)' text-anchor='middle'>Amplitude (mm)</text><text x='55' y='235' font-size='9' text-anchor='end'>0</text><text x='55' y='185' font-size='9' text-anchor='end'>20</text><text x='55' y='135' font-size='9' text-anchor='end'>40</text><text x='55' y='85' font-size='9' text-anchor='end'>60</text></svg>

questions.solution: Amplitude drops from 50 mm at 1 km to 5 mm at 30 km. Total drop = 45 mm over 29 km. Average attenuation ≈ 45/29 ≈ 1.55 mm/km. Closest to 1.8 mm/km (non-linear decay).

questions.stem: If ONGC's Q2 revenue was ₹1,640 Cr and the company expects 12% growth in the next quarter, what will be the projected Q3 revenue?
